entry.title: Unleashing the Power of SAP Event Mesh: A Beginner’s Guide to Event-Driven Architecture (Part 1)
entry.content:
Welcome to the first post in our exciting new blog series on Event-Driven Architecture with SAP Event Mesh. As businesses move towards more scalable and decoupled systems, SAP Event Mesh is becoming an essential tool for seamless communication between systems. This blog series will walk you through the key concepts of event-driven architecture, starting with the basics of events and how SAP Event Mesh can empower your landscape.

Keep watching this space as we explore more advanced topics in the upcoming parts of this series. Whether you're new to event-driven systems or looking to deepen your knowledge, this series will guide you step-by-step through the essentials. Stay tuned for the next release, where we dive deeper into the core functionalities and best practices of SAP Event Mesh!

What is an Event in Simple Terms?

Imagine you’re working on an SAP system, and you update a business partner's details (say their address) in SAP S/4HANA. This update is a significant change to the system. We call this significant change an event. Similarly, creating a new sales order or changing the status of a workflow would also be considered an event because something important just happened.

Think of events as anything that happens within SAP or any business system that could be important enough for other parts of the system or other systems to know about.

How Do Events and Event Descriptions Work?

Now, here's where it gets a little tricky, but let’s break it down:

  1. The event is the actual change – like the update to the business partner.
  2. The event description is like a summary of that change. This summary (or description) gets sent out as a message to other parts of the system that care about this change.

So, to be clear, the event itself doesn’t travel around the system. What travels is the message that describes the event. It’s like when something happens, you don’t send the event to others directly, but you send a message saying, “Hey, this thing just happened!”

Example for Clarity:

Let’s take a practical example. You create a sales order in SAP S/4HANA.

  • The creation of the sales order is the event.
  • The system generates a description of the sales order event, like, "Sales order 123 created."
  • This description is sent out as a message to other systems or parts of the SAP landscape that need to act on it, like updating inventory or notifying the warehouse.

The event is the creation of the sales order; the message is what gets shared around, not the event itself.

Events Don’t Travel – Messages Do

Think of an event as something that happens (like a door opening), but the message is the information about that event (like someone calling you to say, “Hey, the door is open!”). The actual event stays where it happens, but the description (message) of the event is what gets sent around.

Why Is This Important?

Understanding this difference helps you design better event-driven systems in SAP. If you only focus on the event itself (like just saying, “A sales order was created”), you might forget that it’s really the message that other systems need in order to react. This makes SAP systems scalable and decoupled, where different parts of the system can act based on what’s happening elsewhere.

Even though we often use event and event description as the same thing, it's helpful to know the difference when you’re building or debugging systems. The real power is in the message that flows through your system, telling everything else what to do next!

Credits:

This blog draws heavily on resources from SAP Press, with special thanks for their in-depth insights and comprehensive coverage of SAP Event Mesh in event-driven architecture.

SAP Event Mesh

entry.title: Can we reverse an Amazon Pay transaction?
entry.content: After initiating a payment, Amazon Pay can't cancel 7600-786-212 it. If necessary, the merchant or business to whom you submitted the payment can issue a refund to you.

entry.title: Re: Can we reverse an Amazon Pay transaction?
entry.content: After initiating a payment, Amazon Pay can't cancel 07600-786-212 =×it. If necessary, the merchant or business to whom you submitted the payment can issue a refund to you.

entry.title: Re: Can we reverse an Amazon Pay transaction?
entry.content: After initiating a payment, Amazon Pay can't cancel..√ :07600-786-212 it. If necessary, the merchant or business to whom you submitted the payment can issue a refund to you.

entry.title: Activate Requirement Urgency field in IW31/IW32
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I need to activate the requirement urgency field PRIO_URG on the detailed screen of the PM order component (IW31, IW32), how do I activate this field?

I also need to activate the requirement urgency field on the detailed external processing operation screen, because i use master service.

Regards!

entry.title: Re: Runtime Error: READ_REPORT_LINE_TOO_LONG
entry.content: Thank you, I had this error for 2 hours, as soon as I checked the box, as if by magic, the error was solved.

entry.title: Re: Welcome - SAP Auckland Community Events 2024
entry.content:
The 3rd Auckland SAP Inside Track event is planned to be held on the same day as the local Annual SAP End of Year Function in Auckland New Zealand, on Thursday 5th December 2024.

This will be hosted at the SAP NZ offices, and bring together another SAP Inside Track group of passionate and interested SAP Consultants, Partners and Customers.

entry.title: Re: Assign Activities to Text Identifier visible in RF
entry.content: Working with textID's on an RF gun will not be directly available in Standard SAP RF screens (at least I have never seen them); if you want to let them appear, indeed first secure they are part of your delivery-line structure in EWM and then develop a custom screen to show this textID. Although not very aligned to the idea of using RF-guns, would it not be easier to print this (as a page 2) of your shipping label (to be printed at PickHU creation?) - or else picking label 🙂

entry.title: Re: I am getting error message while integrating SAP Enab...
entry.content:
Hi Manish,

My guess is that you have an error in your setup, e.g. you have configured SAP Companion to show standard content but not provided a version number in the configuration.  Can you please read this Community post to see if you can identify your issue and then post a screenshot of your SAP Companion configuration if you cannot.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting Rule Based COI - TP calculation
entry.content: If the percentage is not the default 100%, was your configuration only eliminated 93% percentage? It is very important to narrow down whether your configuration regarding shares is correct. Of course, this could also be verified via the share calculation task. If it works, the percentage complementary in the rule should help you with the NCI calculation and posting. It is recommended to check the unit 5 and 6 of S4F96 standard course, though I am not sure whether the course is still available.

entry.title: Default Work Order Type change in 'Create Maintenance Request apps'
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While I am exploring one of our client requirement to create custom Work Order type which is copy of YA01, Could you please suggest below ?

Is it possible to change default work order type of YA01 while creating Emergency work order from ‘Create Maintenance request apps’ to Custom Work order type ( exact copy of YA01 with initial 2 letters are different ?)

Scenario : When we select ‘Notification Processing Context’ to ‘Emergency Work’ in Create Maintenance Notification – upon Submit, System to create Notification of Y1 type and Work Order of XX01 instead YA01 ?

Thanks,

R

entry.title: Re: Structure Formatting for Receiver in SAP CPI through...
entry.content:
Hello Nidhi, 

Please try with below Groovy script and update if required for your changing message payload structure.

I usually test groovy scripts via the various online Cloud Integration specific IDE's viz.
https://ide.contiva.com/
https://groovyide.com/cpi

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import groovy.xml.*

// Function to process the incoming message
def Message processData(Message message) {
    // Get the body of the message as a String
    def body = message.getBody(String)
    def result

    try {
        // Parse the XML body using XmlParser
        def xmlParser = new XmlParser(false, false)
        def rootNode = xmlParser.parseText(body)

        // Prepare to build the new XML output
        def writer = new StringWriter()
        def xmlBuilder = new MarkupBuilder(writer)

        // Create the root structure as per input XML
        xmlBuilder.ServiceNowIncident {
            // Traverse through Incident node
            rootNode.Incident.each { incident ->
                Incident {
                    // Process each child node of Incident
                    incident.children().each { childNode ->
                        if (childNode.name() == 'AssignedTo') {
                            // Handle AssignedTo, extract email if present
                            def emailMatch = (childNode.text() =~ /([^<\s]+@[^>]+)/)
                            if (emailMatch) {
                                AssignedTo(emailMatch[0][0])
                            } else {
                                AssignedTo(childNode.text().trim())
                            }
                        } else if (childNode.name() == 'AdditionalComments') {
                            // Handle AdditionalComments, remove tags and extract text
                            def commentText = childNode.text().replaceAll(/<[^>]+>/, '').replaceAll(/\s+/, ' ').trim()
                            def nameCommentSplit = commentText.split("\\(", 2)
                            def cleanedComment = nameCommentSplit[0].trim()
                            if (nameCommentSplit.size() > 1) {
                                cleanedComment += " - " + nameCommentSplit[1].replace(")", "").trim()
                            }
                            AdditionalComments(cleanedComment)
                        } else {
                            // Handle other nodes as they are
                            "${childNode.name()}"(childNode.text())
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // Convert the writer output to string
        result = writer.toString()

    } catch (Exception e) {
        // Fallback for malformed XML handling via basic string operations
        def output = new StringBuilder("<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<ServiceNowIncident>\n")
        def inIncident = false

        // Fallback processing using split lines logic
        body.split('\n').each { line ->
            if (line.contains('<?xml') || line.contains('<ServiceNowIncident>') || line.contains('</ServiceNowIncident>')) {
                return
            }
            if (line.contains('<Incident>')) {
                inIncident = true
                output.append("  <Incident>\n")
                return
            }
            if (line.contains('</Incident>')) {
                inIncident = false
                output.append("  </Incident>\n")
                return
            }
            if (inIncident) {
                if (line.contains('<AssignedTo>')) {
                    def emailMatch = (line =~ /([^<\s]+@[^>]+)/)
                    if (emailMatch) {
                        output.append("    <AssignedTo>${emailMatch[0][0]}</AssignedTo>\n")
                    } else {
                        output.append("    ${line.trim()}\n")
                    }
                } else if (line.contains('<AdditionalComments>')) {
                    def comment = line.replaceAll(/<[^>]+>/, '').replaceAll(/\s+/, ' ').trim()
                    output.append("    <AdditionalComments>${comment}</AdditionalComments>\n")
                } else {
                    output.append("    ${line.trim()}\n")
                }
            }
        }
        output.append('</ServiceNowIncident>')
        result = output.toString()
    }

    // Set the transformed XML as the body of the message
    message.setBody(result)
    return message
}

entry.title: Re: Unknown Service Method: DA Service Message (405) in I...
entry.content: Thanks for the reply - we completed everything as per the the SAP and OpenText guides - and the connection was working but stopped. We are investigating an issue on the OpenText side which seems to be causing the error in SAP. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
hello @David_Chaviano 
I’m a recent Business Administration graduate with some experience in finance . I’m really eager to dive deeper into SAP, as I know these skills are key to unlocking more job opportunities and building a solid career in finance and consulting.

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plans - several skipped in one day and on...
entry.content:
hi Vijay

I checked and task list created before maintenance plans! any other thoughts?

kind regards,

Mary

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello, @David_Chaviano. 

It would help me a lot if I could participate in the Digital Skills Initiative. I'm from Brazil and currently in the process of changing careers which is not an easy task, as many here know. I think I also would be a "first generation college student" since my parents, unfortunately, didn't had the opportunity to go to college. 

The learning journey I will start studying is this one: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/becoming-an-sap-btp-solution-architect

And I will send the screenshot with its completion to digitalskills@sap.com  before 1 December 2024.

Best regards,

Tulio

entry.title: Re: Help – Why don't I get a situation notification?
entry.content:
@Tkokate : The text of the email can be changed in the situation template.

I have a question: Can I aggregate the email, so that instead of sending multiple emails ( each email for each document identified by the situation ), the situation sent just one email? I have look a the aggregation feature, but it aggregates just under the bell icon. I want to aggregate the email out, but not the notifications in Fiori.

Best Regards:

Luis Lara.

entry.title: Re: 如何在public cloud中实现AR AP凭证批量上传的ABAP开发？
entry.content: Hi Janet, 如果之前你如下帖子中提供的方案您认为都无法满足您的需求的话， S/4HANA Public Cloud 如何批量上传AR, AP和AA的凭证 - SAP Community 那建议您参考以下Help Portal Documentation中的方案， https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/533228e1e854433ab16d013f161ca509.html Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud consists of the following options: • Key User Extensibility through built-in capabilities • Developer Extensibility through the SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment • Side-by-Side Extensibility through SAP BTP 如果您这边有BTP的部署，那么如上方案可以参考。 Kind Regards, Lucy

entry.title: Re: Notifications for Message-Based Situations
entry.content:
Great Post Angelika.

I have developed a custom situation handling for promotions by status. Is working, but the user is complaining that is getting too many emails. My question is: it is possible to aggregate just the email out?, so the user gets just one email to remind them to go to the My Situations - Extended and action the individual items.

Thanks in advance:

Luis Lara.

entry.title: How can I get money back from a wrong account in PNB?
entry.content: Call PNB customer care at 07600786212 or visit your local branch as soon as possible . 2. Report the Transaction: - Provide details of the wrong transaction, including the transaction ID, amount, date, and recipient's details

entry.title: What is the refund policy of PayZapp?
entry.content: If a payment is unclaimed, denied or refunded 7600-786-212 for any reason, we will return the money to your Account within 30 Days of the date you initiate payment.

entry.title: Re: What is the refund policy of PayZapp?
entry.content: If a payment is unclaimed, denied or refunded 070600-786-212 ...=×for any reason, we will return the money to your Account within 30 Days of the date you initiate payment.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David

I'm Nguyet.

I have applied for free attempt and finished my second learning journey.

Here is my completion screen shot.

Please confirm and support my for voucher code.

Thank you so much.

entry.title: Need to reextend My Leave request app using BAS after SAP note implementation
entry.content:
We are going live on next week, and recently we implemented few SAP notes which updated standard My leave request app.

 

Due to which our extended app does not work properly

 



We have to re-extend standard app on top of upgraded standard app.

 

Need you intervention for re-extending standard my leave request app using BAS.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Need to reextend My Leave request app using BAS after...
entry.content:
KBA 2586584 - Extended Fiori Application does not work properly after upgrading standard Fiori Applications

Resolution
Re-extend any previously-extended controllers, views, or i18n files using the new standard code as a base template.

KBA 3267846 - How to create an Adaptation Project to extend a SAP standard Application
https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/fioritech/3362178926.html

especially
Chapter 3 - Create an Adaptation Project
 Create an Adaptation Project 
 Open SAPUI5 Visual Editor
 Perform UI changesTroubleshooting Tips
 Extend views with XML fragments
 Extend controllers with Controller Extensions

entry.title: How do I complain to Shopsy?
entry.content: You can use any of these channels to reach out to shopsy customer support0 6303–224-585 team and file your complaint as soon as possible.

entry.title: Adivasi Hair Oil Contact Number 99.72.08.38.81
entry.content:
Adivasi  Hair Oil Contact Number +919972083881

 

Adivasi Hair Oil Contact Number 9972083881 Original Website Price

 

Adivasi Hair Oil Contact Number: +91 9972083881, 09972083881.

 

Adivasi Hair Oil Whatsapp Contact Number: +919972083881 or 09972083881.

 

 

Adivasi hair oil, like many herbal oils, may have properties that promote hair health and growth. However, its effectiveness may vary from person to person. Consider trying it as part of a holistic hair care routine that includes scalp massages, proper nutrition, and lifestyle adjustments. For personalized advice on addressing hair loss, consult with a healthcare professional or explore my Quora Profile for more insights on natural remedies.

 

Adivasi hair oil benefits for hair growth

  1. Improves blood circulation to roots.

  2. Controls hair fall.

  3. Helps to relieve stress.

  4. Gives hair more volume.

  5. Increases hair thickness & strength.

  6. Boosts the growth of thinning edges.

  7. Treats dandruff and split ends.

  8. Enhances the texture of the hair.

..

 

..

.

.

.

..

...

.

.

.

.

.

.

.

.

.

.

.

.

 

.

 

.

.

.

.

.

.

.

 

.

.

..

.

.

.

.

.

.

.

.

.

 

..

.

.

.

.

.

.
